author: ducktapekiller
description: Highlight text and add tags in Reading View. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: DuckTapeKiller
manifest.description: Highlight text and add tags in Reading View.
manifest.name: Reader Highlighter Tags

name: Reader Highlighter Tags

releases.author: DuckTapeKiller
releases.description:
## Reader Highlighter Tags 1.0.5

**Fixes**

- Cleared all remaining items flagged by the Obsidian plugin review (redundant type assertions and deprecated API usage), with no change to features or appearance.
- Settings tab now re-renders through an internal helper instead of the deprecated `display()` method.
- Destructive buttons keep their styling without the deprecated `setWarning()` API.

**Notes**

- No functional changes versus 1.0.4; this is a code-quality and review-compliance release.
- Requires Obsidian 1.7.2 or newer.

releases.author: DuckTapeKiller
releases.description:
## Reader Highlighter Tags 1.0.4

**Changes**
- Default hotkeys removed. All actions remain available as commands; assign your own hotkeys in Obsidian's Hotkeys settings. Existing custom bindings are unaffected.
- UI labels in the command palette and settings updated to sentence case.
- Clipboard copy now uses the standard Clipboard API only (removed the deprecated `execCommand` fallback).
- Minimum Obsidian version is now 1.7.2.

**Internal**
- Entire codebase converted to typed TypeScript with no behavioral changes to highlighting, tagging, annotations, exports, or the navigator.
- Added CI and release pipelines (lint, format, tests, build).
- README cleanup.

releases.author: DuckTapeKiller
releases.description:
## Reader Highlighter Tags 1.0.3

**New**
- Remove annotations directly from the Highlight Navigator: "Remove annotation" (single) and "Remove all annotations (note)", plus a new "Remove all annotations from note" command.
- "Copy" action added to the right-click menu for both highlights and footnotes in the Navigator.

**Fixes**
- Navigator list now refreshes immediately after removing a highlight or annotation (previously it kept showing removed items until reload).
- Clicking a footnote in the Navigator now scrolls to the footnote definition instead of stopping at the paragraph.
- Footnote numbers in the Navigator now match the numbers shown in Reading View (Obsidian renumbers footnotes sequentially), so the panel and the note stay consistent.
- Unreferenced (orphan) footnote definitions are hidden for grouped-notes style notes, keeping numbering clean without affecting normally numbered notes.

**Other**
- Minimum Obsidian version raised to 1.7.2 to match the APIs the plugin uses.

releases.author: DuckTapeKiller
releases.description:
## 🚀 Key Features
### 📝 Inline Highlight Editor
No need to switch to edit mode to adjust a highlight. You can now edit existing highlights directly from the **Highlight Navigator** sidebar:
* **Style Toggling**: Instantly convert highlights between the default `==` syntax and HTML `<mark>` syntax.
* **Tag Modification**: Directly edit or fuzzy-pick tags prepended to the highlight.
* **Color Tuning**: Swap semantic colors using the 15-color palette or enter a custom hex color code.
* **Footnote Annotations**: Edit or add footnote comments associated with the highlight.
* **Direct Deletion**: Remove a highlight markup altogether with a single click.
### 🧹 Bulk & Layout Utilities
A new **Bulk** action menu has been added to the sidebar footer to keep your notes clean and consistent:
* **Merge Adjacent Highlights**: Automatically collapses neighboring highlights of the same type/color into a single combined highlight if they are only separated by whitespace.
* **Bulk Recolor**: Replace a specific color (or all colors) across all `<mark>` highlights in your document with a new target color in one go.
* **Legacy Migration**: Automatically converts old `<span>` based highlights into the standard, theme-friendly HTML `<mark>` syntax.
### 📊 Multi-Format Exports
In addition to Markdown, you can now export your highlights in structured data formats. Right-click the **Export** button in the sidebar to export as:
* **Markdown (.md)** (with live transclusions)
* **JSON (.json)** (ideal for developers and custom integrations)
* **CSV (.csv)** (perfect for importing into spreadsheets or database tools)
### 🎨 Local Canvas Export
* Added a dedicated **Canvas** button to the Highlight Navigator footer. Export the highlights of your current active note directly into a structured Obsidian Canvas node graph with a single click.
---
## 🔧 Under the Hood
* **Refactored Parser (`highlights.js`)**: Isolated and consolidated all AST-like parsing and CRUD operations for Markdown/HTML highlights to ensure stability and dry code.
* **Robust Single-Step Undo**: Streamlined state storage to safely preserve a lightweight snapshot of your note prior to modifications, enabling instant one-step recovery.
* **Clean Formatting**: Added support for `.prettierrc` standards and improved consistency in whitespace collapsing when parsing.

releases.author: DuckTapeKiller
releases.description:
Fixed:

Previously, the highlights on the Navigator showed Markdown syntax. Now they are shown clean. 

Improvements:

On mobile, now the navigator collapses after you tap on any highlight or footnote so that you can read it inside the note.
